content:
- Deep-verified and then executed story-pgbackrest-managed-backup-recovery/01: added backup-provider abstraction + pgBackRest integration wiring.
- Implemented `src/backup/*` (typed ops + pgBackRest renderer + runtime-facing job builders) and process-worker integration (new job kinds/specs + build_command + timeout mapping + unit tests + real-binary `pgbackrest --version` smoke).
- Extended config v2 schema with `[backup]` block, added `process.backup_timeout_ms`, added `process.binaries.pgbackrest` as optional (required when `backup.enabled=true`), and added `BinaryPathsV2Input` so missing fields stay actionable `ConfigError::Validation.field` instead of serde parse errors.
- Updated harness/provenance: added `expected_version.kind=pgbackrest` (major check), updated remediation strings, added pgBackRest to real-binaries policy, and added hardened `tools/install-pgbackrest.sh`.
- Installer gotcha: AlmaLinux aarch64 lacked `pgbackrest` in base repos and PGDG package depended on `libssh2` (not available until enabling EPEL). Enabled EPEL repo and used PGDG repo RPM bootstrap path; script updated to auto-enable `epel`.
- Updated docs (operator config + quick-start + contributor testing/harness docs) to document pgBackRest and `[backup]` config semantics.
- Gates all green: `make check`, `make test`, `make test-long`, `make lint`.

- Read `.ralph/current_tasks.md` to determine next work item.
- Verified no bug tasks are currently failing (`<passes>false</passes>` / `<passing>false</passing>` absent under `.ralph/tasks/bugs`).
- Found a required recurring meta-task (`<passes>meta-task</passes>`): `.ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/18-task-recurring-meta-deep-skeptical-codebase-review.md`.
- Wrote that task path to `.ralph/current_task.txt` as mandated by the META-TASK CHECK rule.
- Next: switch into do-task mode to execute the chosen meta-task.

- Processed PO mail: "It picked 18 again" from user@toffemail.nl.
- Followed required workflow: sent immediate ack email, sent planning/status emails during inspection, and sent final summary email.
- Checked `.ralph/current_tasks.md` (underscore filename exists; there is no `.ralph/current tasks.md`).
- Found task 18 block around lines 539-546.
- Observed explicit low-priority signals already present in current_tasks alone:
  - header has `<priority>very_low</priority>`
  - header/title says recurring meta-task
  - line says `NEVER TICK OFF THIS TASK...`
  - description starts `This is a RECURRING META-TASK.`
- Decision: no change required because the file already clearly marks task 18 as very low priority recurring meta-task.
- No codebase edits made.
- If this gets picked again later, next investigation should be task-picker weighting/rules rather than task-18 labeling.

- Processed PO mail: "Read choose prompt" from user@toffemail.nl.
- Followed required frequent-email workflow: sent immediate ack, plan/update, subtask-start update, subtask-complete with direct answer, and final summary email.
- Investigated chooser prompt directly in `.ralph/ralph-choose-task.md` and confirmed call path from `.ralph/ralph-worker.sh`.
- Verified exact wording: meta-task is mandatory over `<passes>true</passes>` tasks, but `<passes>false</passes>` tasks (and especially failing bug tasks via bug-first rule) take precedence over meta-task.
- No code or task file edits were made.
- Next: await PO follow-up; if asked about mis-picks, inspect ordering logic against current_tasks states.

- Processed PO follow-up mail: "Make it more clear to always prefer non passing tasks over meta-task".
- Followed mandatory frequent-email workflow and sent multiple updates: immediate ack, plan, subtask start, issue note, subtask completion, final summary.
- Read `.ralph/ralph-choose-task.md` and confirmed order logic was correct but wording could be misread.
- Edited `.ralph/ralph-choose-task.md` only:
  - added strict precedence list (failing bugs -> other non-passing -> meta-task only if zero non-passing -> passing)
  - strengthened non-passing branch to state it always outranks meta-task
  - rewrote meta-task check to be explicitly gated by zero non-passing tasks and fail-closed.
- Small snag: shell quoting in one rg command due backticks; resolved immediately and reported in status email.
- Did not touch code outside requested prompt file; no task creation was needed because PO explicitly asked for this direct prompt wording change.
- Next if this still mis-picks in practice: inspect runtime chooser implementation/invocation state (`current_tasks` parsing and branch checks), not just prompt wording.

- Inspected `docs/src/interfaces/node-api.md` against `src/api/worker.rs` for route parity.
- Found documented endpoints: GET /ha/state, POST /switchover, DELETE /ha/switchover, and optional debug GET /debug/ui, /debug/verbose, /debug/snapshot.
- Found implemented routes include all above and also GET /fallback/cluster and POST /fallback/heartbeat in `route_request`.
- No documented endpoint appears to be missing from implementation in these two files.
- Next: decide whether /fallback/* should be documented in node-api (or removed/relocated) to restore route parity depending on intended public surface.

- Verified docs claims for subagent slices 04-06 under .ralph/evidence/meta-18-pass7-20260304T205520Z.
- Extracted all 21 rows (17 unique claim IDs), then mapped each to concrete src/tests evidence with line-level anchors.
- Confirmed endpoint claims in src/api/worker.rs and controller projection in src/api/controller.rs, including /fallback/heartbeat, /ha/state, /switchover, /debug/ui, /debug/verbose.
- Confirmed backup option safety guard (`--stanza*`/`--repo*` blocked) is implemented in both config parser and backup renderer, with unit test in src/backup/pgbackrest.rs.
- Confirmed HA snapshot claim by code structure: ha::worker builds world from subscribers; ha::decide is pure over DecideInput; trust/pg split evidenced in dcs/state + pginfo worker + ha decide.
- Confirmed harness port-collision claim via src/test_harness/ports.rs layout + uniqueness tests.
- Found several policy/process claims are not mechanically enforced in code/tests (docs-style / verification language rules / canonical terminology consistency / per-page claim logging); marked unsupported-by-code-tests.
- Found `process.binaries` troubleshooting claim is only partially supported: non-empty/required validation exists and spawn errors surface at runtime, but no runtime preflight executability check in config validation.
- Attempted to run targeted proof tests, but `cargo test --lib -- --list` failed to compile due unrelated existing errors in src/test_harness/provenance.rs (missing functions verify_policy_optional_pins / verify_attestation_metadata / verify_attested_entry_metadata).
- Next if continuing: decide whether to file follow-up bug tasks for unsupported policy claims and binary executability overstatement, then rerun proof tests after provenance compile issue is fixed.

- Verified slices 13-15 claims by reading all referenced docs and tracing runtime code/tests anchors.
- Extracted every row from slice-13/14/15 and mapped each to code/tests or policy-only docs.
- Found one concrete overstatement/outdated claim: task-33 report line says redirect stubs exist in docs/src, but files docs/src/architecture.md, components.md, glossary.md, operations.md are missing.
- Ran targeted cargo test attempt for HA/API evidence, but current workspace failed to compile before tests due existing unresolved functions in src/test_harness/provenance.rs (verify_policy_optional_pins / verify_attestation_metadata / verify_attested_entry_metadata).
- Prepared verdict set: verified, partial, or outdated per row, with rewrite suggestions for overstated lines (especially /ha/state "responds consistently" and broad "architectural core" phrasing).
- Next if continuing: optionally patch docs/src/verification/task-33-docs-verification-report.md to remove stale "These exist in docs/src" section or rewrite as historical note.

- Verified doc claims for slice-07/08/09 from .ralph/evidence/meta-18-pass7-20260304T205520Z/subagents.
- Read all cited doc anchors and code anchors (api worker/controller, cli client, runtime startup planner, ha decide, etcd harness, provenance checks).
- Ran targeted tests for high-priority areas:
  - cargo test --lib api::worker::tests::security_legacy_auth_token_fallback_protects_ha_routes -- --exact (PASS)
  - cargo test --lib ha::decide::tests::transition_matrix_cases -- --exact (PASS)
  - cargo test --lib runtime::node::tests::select_startup_mode_prefers_clone_when_foreign_healthy_leader_exists -- --exact (PASS)
  - cargo test --lib test_harness::provenance::tests::provenance_rejects_checksum_mismatch -- --exact (PASS)
- Main outcomes:
  - Endpoint/auth/safety claims are mostly correct and supported by code/tests.
  - Found concrete docs drift:
    1) harness-internals readiness wording says client-port connect check, but code now also does etcd KV round-trip readiness.
    2) docs-style universal deep-dive chapter structure claim is violated by at least codebase-map chapter.
- Created grouped bug task using add-bug skill instructions:
  - .ralph/tasks/bugs/docs-claims-drift-harness-readiness-and-chapter-shape.md
- Additional nuance found:
  - task-33 verification report rows in slices reference old docs paths under docs/src/architecture/*. The behavior claims can still be validated in code, but report doc_loc references are stale after docs reorganization.
- Next after context reset:
  - If asked, patch contributor docs + verification report for path freshness and wording accuracy, then run docs gate checks.

- Verified doc-claim slices 01-03 by reading all 20 rows from `.ralph/evidence/meta-18-pass7-20260304T205520Z/subagents/slice-{01,02,03}.txt` and mapping each anchor to concrete code/tests.
- Pulled exact supporting symbols with line refs across API worker/controller, runtime wiring, debug snapshot/view worker, CLI client, config parser/schema, backup pgBackRest renderer, HA decide, pginfo worker/state, DCS state, and harness modules.
- Ran focused proof tests (all passed) after correcting an initial 0-test filter mistake by preflighting with `cargo test --lib -- --list` and then using full exact test names:
  - `api::worker::tests::ha_state_route_returns_503_without_subscriber`
  - `api::worker::tests::security_role_permissions_allow_read_deny_admin`
  - `ha::decide::tests::transition_matrix_cases`
  - `config::parser::tests::load_runtime_config_v2_happy_path_with_safe_defaults`
  - `backup::pgbackrest::tests::render_backup_forbids_stanza_override_in_options`
  - plus endpoint/contract checks in `api::worker::tests::debug_ui_route_returns_html_scaffold`, `api::controller::{post_switchover_writes_typed_record_to_expected_key,delete_switchover_deletes_expected_key}`, and `worker_contract_tests::step_once_contracts_are_callable`.
- Identified policy-only/high-risk docs-style/verification rows (`cl0014`, `cl0017`, `cl0031`, `cl0056`) as not code/test-enforced invariants; Makefile docs-lint currently enforces fenced-block policy via `tools/docs-architecture-no-code-guard.sh`, not claim-truth/evidence-anchor completeness.
- Next: deliver user-facing row-by-row verdict matrix with high-severity rows first, each including verdict, evidence paths+symbols, and concrete commands to reproduce.

- Verified slices 10-12 by reading claim anchors and tracing to src/tests/tooling.
- Collected concrete anchors for API switchover delete route, startup mode healthy-leader selection, rewind->bootstrap fallback, config identity matching, docs/book hygiene guard, stress artifact+shutdown finalization, and HA lease TTL guardrail.
- Ran helper comparisons for contributors read-order vs SUMMARY (matches when flattened to include nested Harness Internals) and coverage-checklist vs SUMMARY for task-33 report (drift/staleness found: checklist does not cover many current pages).
- Ran `make docs-hygiene` successfully.
- Attempted targeted cargo verification for provenance claim; got inconsistent failures in this dirty/in-flight workspace (first compile error looked like missing helper fns, rerun hit known linker object disappearance flake). Logged a bug file for the observed compile issue per repo instruction, but noted workspace edits are in-flight and evidence is unstable.
- Next if continuing: rerun targeted tests in a stabilized tree (`cargo clean`, maybe `CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0`) and resolve provenance compile/link instability before treating real-binary provenance claims as fully gate-verified at runtime.
